sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: How GM Will Steer Manufacturing Through Global Volatility
feed.summary: General Motors is adapting its manufacturing strategy to global volatility, a cooling EV market, and shifting trade policies. The company is restructuring operations, moving production of certain models back to the US, and balancing its long-term all-electric vision with the current need for a broader range of powertrains, including hybrids. These adjustments come alongside significant financial charges related to EV valuation and Chinese operations, as GM focuses on internal efficiencies and advanced driver-assist technologies.

feed.title: AppOmni Uncovers Agentic AI Security Vulnerability in ServiceNow’s API and Agents
feed.summary: AppOmni's Chief of Security Research, Aaron Costello, uncovered a critical "BodySnatcher" vulnerability in ServiceNow's Virtual Agent API and Now Assist AI Agents. This flaw allowed unauthenticated attackers to impersonate any ServiceNow user and bypass security controls due to a shared secret and simple email-based account linking, enabling the creation of backdoor accounts with full privileges. ServiceNow has since patched the vulnerability, and AppOmni recommends best practices for agentic AI systems, including strong provider configuration controls, automated agent approval, and disabling unused agents, to prevent similar exploits.

feed.title: Is Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) Still Attractive After CASGEVY Progress And A Higher P/E Ratio
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) is currently trading at US$454.67, with recent attention on its pipeline progress, including CASGEVY. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 38.1%, indicating an intrinsic value of about $734.21 per share. However, its current P/E ratio of 31.39x is higher than the biotech industry average, causing the company to appear overvalued by this metric, prompting investors to consider various valuation narratives.

feed.title: Analysts Expect Breakeven For MDxHealth SA (NASDAQ:MDXH) Before Long
feed.summary: Analysts are predicting that MDxHealth SA (NASDAQ:MDXH), a commercial-stage precision diagnostics company, will reach breakeven and turn a profit in 2027. The company posted a loss of US$38m in its most recent financial year, but is expected to grow its revenue by 81% year-on-year to achieve profitability. Investors should note the company currently has negative equity on its balance sheet.

feed.title: Target revamp: Variety of improvements planned for store on Southside's Whiskey Road
feed.summary: Target's store on Whiskey Road in Aiken, South Carolina, is undergoing a major refurbishment set to be completed by summer. The revamp will introduce a larger grocery section with fresh produce, updated décor, enhanced digital services, dedicated display spaces for Disney and Apple, and improved restrooms and a nursing area. These upgrades are part of Target's ongoing investment in modernizing its guest experience.

feed.title: Dan Ives Predicts Tesla Will Own '80%' Of Robotaxi Market — Says TSLA In His Top 5 List For 2026
feed.summary: Dan Ives of Wedbush Securities reiterates his bullish outlook on Tesla, predicting the company will dominate 80% of the Robotaxi market by 2026. He places Tesla at the top of his personal list of top five stocks for the year, alongside Microsoft, Apple, Palantir, and CrowdStrike. Ives's prediction comes despite Tesla's Robotaxi service missing previous self-imposed deadlines and the strong performance of competitors like Waymo.

Following this transaction, Array's Board of Directors has declared a special cash dividend of $10.25 per common share payable on February 2, 2026, to shareholders of record on January 23, 2026. This move returns significant value to shareholders, building on the company's financial strategy.

feed.title: United States Bronchiolitis Obliterans Syndrome Treatment Market Size, Trends, Growth drivers | Top Companies - Zambon, Incyte Corporation, Genentech
feed.summary: The Bronchiolitis Obliterans Syndrome (BOS) treatment market is projected to grow significantly due to novel therapies, precision medicine, and the rising prevalence of post-transplant complications. Key players like Zambon, Incyte Corporation, and Genentech are driving innovation in immunomodulators, biologics, and supportive care. The market is segmented by drug type (immunosuppressive drugs, biologics, antibiotics) and end-user (hospitals, specialty clinics), with North America leading in market share.

Analysts foresee a substantial upside, especially as the broader healthcare sector is undervalued.

feed.title: Kroger reshapes senior retail leadership with promotions and retirements
feed.summary: Kroger has announced significant changes to its senior retail leadership, including promotions and retirements. Victor Smith has been promoted to senior vice-president of retail divisions, Monica Garnes will lead the Atlanta division, and Ken DeLuca will preside over the Fry’s division. The company also announced the retirements of Tom Schwilke and Colleen Juergensen, with Kendra Doyel succeeding Schwilke.

feed.title: Coca-Cola abandons plans to sell Costa Coffee - report
feed.summary: Coca-Cola has reportedly dropped its plans to sell Costa Coffee after private equity bids failed to meet its expectations, with the auction process ending in December. The company had been seeking around £2 billion for the coffee chain, which is roughly half of what it paid in 2018. This decision comes as Coca-Cola prepares for a change in leadership, though a future sale has not been ruled out.
